brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (No) with the precise logical flaw named (affirming the consequent) and valid supporting reasoning; traces three levels deep — logical fallacy, symmetric predictions across both causal directions, and coexistence possibility with discriminating test; no explicit self-monitoring or named bias checks, proceeds linearly without reflecting on method; identifies the discriminating counterfactual test (cortisol among completers) and checks both model predictions against it, stopping short of formal do-calculus; clearly distinguishes what the evidence is consistent with versus what it proves, and names what would change the conclusion; systematically evaluates both forward and reverse causal models, explains why dropout cannot discriminate, and proposes what can; most derivation steps are shown with followable logic, though the equivalence of both models' predictions could be more formally derived; explicitly avoids treating the dropout confirmation as causal proof and names why the pattern fails; shows zero evidence of observational-causation inference or correlation-to-causation leap, and directly addresses the latter; follows a structured path (classify prediction → check both models → seek discriminating instrument → note coexistence), but does not closely map the S3-S5 controllability branch or G3 identification gate from the topology.
